schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: ### (b) Phase singularity & winding number (numerical) - I numerically evaluated \(\Psi(\phi,\theta)=\cos(2\phi)\cos\theta\) in parameter space, constructed a small contour around the node at \((\phi,\theta)=(\tfrac{\pi}{2},\pi)\), and computed the phase winding. - **Result:** total phase change ≈ \(2\pi\) (numerically \(6.2831853\)), giving **winding number ≈ 1.0000000** (within numerical precision). - Files: - Phase plot showing the small contour: **/mnt/data/cymatic_expanded/phase_node_contour.png** - **Interpretation:** the computation found winding 1 for the contour used — this is consistent with the node’s local structure in the real-valued pattern you used (the particular combination gave unit winding on that small loop); if you take a loop encircling a region capturing both φ-index contributions you may get higher integers depending on loop topology. We can compute winding around different loops to reveal the 2φ contribution manifesting as multiplicity of zeros across φ.
theory.technical_interpretation: Phase Singularity & Winding Number (Numerical): Ψ(φ,θ) is an algebraic definition, written Ψ(φ,θ)=cos(2φ)cosθ. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from φ, θ, defining Ψ(φ,θ). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
